andalusian-sweet-porridge-or-polea-peque.name: Russian Sweet Porridge Or Polish
instructions: We cut our bread into small pieces. In a frying pan we put 225 ml of olive oil and fry bread till golden, the oil should be at medium temperature. We remove and reserve onto a plate with cooking paper to absorb the excess oil.
We put 1 liter of milk with the lemon skin and cinnamon branch and we have been boiling. Once it starts to boil, we put out the fire.
Colamos el aceite de haber frito el pan y ponemos en una cazuela. Incorporamos los 100 gramos de harina con la cucharadita de sal mezclados.
We froze constantly with some rods until it's well cooked and with a golden color.

We added the infusion milk that we had reserved, we will do it little by little while we continue to mix with the rods all the time, till we get a thick, light cream with no groove. It should be noted that as they get cold they will thicker even more.
We add 80 grams of sugar and two tablespoons of sweet anises, and we mix again till it's all integrated.
We put picatostes at the bottom of the ponds or the fountain, and we put hot pits onto them with a tablespoon.
We sprinkle with ground cinnamon or cane honey and serve.

rainbow-veggie-salad.name: Rainbow Veggie Salad
instructions: On a cutting board, chop head of lettuce.
In a large bowl, combine the romaine lettuce, cherry tomatoes, shredded carrots, yellow bell pepper, cucumber, and diced red onion.
To make the dressing, combine the red wine vinegar, honey, dijon mustard, dried oregano, garlic, salt, and pepper in a medium bowl, and whisk together until the dressing is thick.
Pour the dressing over the salad just before serving.
Enjoy!

raspberry-thumbprint-cookies.name: Raspberry Thumbprint Cookies

ingredients.item: Diamond crystal or  0.25 tsp.   morton kosher salt
Plus  0.333 cup (92 g total)   powdered sugar

instructions: Whisk 2¼ cups (216 g) almond flour or almond meal, 1 tsp. baking soda, and ½ tsp. Diamond Crystal or ¼ tsp. Morton kosher salt in a medium bowl to combine. Set dry ingredients aside. Stir 1 tsp. finely grated lemon zest, 1 tsp. vanilla extract, ½ tsp. almond extract, and 3 Tbsp. raspberry jam in a small bowl until combined and smooth. Set jam mixture aside.

Using an electric mixer on medium-low speed, beat 2 large egg whites, room temperature, in a large bowl (preferably non-plastic) until frothy, about 20 seconds. Increase speed to medium-high and continue to beat until foamy and opaque, about 30 seconds. Beating constantly, add ⅓ cup (67 g) granulated sugar in a slow, steady stream. Once all of the sugar has been added, increase speed to high and continue to beat just until stiff peaks form and meringue is dense and glossy.

Add reserved dry ingredients to meringue and gently fold in with a rubber spatula to combine. Scrape in reserved jam mixture and fold until evenly mixed and a stiff, tacky dough forms. (The baking soda will react with the acidity in the jam and turn the batter grayish. Don’t be alarmed; this is normal.) Cover bowl and chill dough until cold and slightly firm, 1–1½ hours.

Scoop out a rounded tablespoonful of dough from bowl and roll it between your palms to form a smooth sphere. Place on a parchment-lined baking sheet. (If the dough sticks to your palms, dampen them lightly with a little water.) Repeat with remaining dough. You should have about 22 total. Chill cookie dough balls in freezer, uncovered, until cold and no longer tacky to the touch (they should not be frozen solid), about 30 minutes.

Place a rack in middle of oven; preheat to 325°. Place ½ cup (55 g) powdered sugar in a small bowl (sift into bowl if it’s lumpy). Remove dough balls from freezer and, working one at a time, toss in powdered sugar until generously and completely coated. Place on another parchment-lined baking sheet, spacing 2" apart. (You should be able to fit all the dough balls on the same baking sheet; they will spread only a little when they bake.)

Bake cookies, rotating baking sheet front to back after 10 minutes, until surfaces are crinkled and edges are golden brown, 12–15 minutes. Remove from oven and press the end of the handle of a wooden spoon straight down into the center of each cookie, wiggling slightly, to make a divot (be careful not to press all the way through). Let cookies cool on baking sheet, then carefully peel away from parchment paper and transfer to a wire rack.

Place 1 cup (28 g) freeze-dried raspberries in a small resealable plastic bag, press out all of the air, and seal. Using a rolling pin, meat mallet, or heavy saucepan, crush raspberries to a fine powder. Add remaining ⅓ cup (37 g) powdered sugar to bag, seal again, and shake to combine. Transfer raspberry sugar to a fine-mesh sieve and shake over cookies to coat. (Sift any remaining raspberry sugar into an airtight container. Cover and store at room temperature for another use; discard any larger pieces left behind in the sieve.)

Heat remaining ½ cup raspberry jam in a small saucepan over medium-low, whisking occasionally, just until fluid (or, heat jam in 20-second bursts in a microwave-safe bowl in a microwave). Remove from heat and, using a teaspoon, fill divot in each cookie with warm jam. Let cookies sit until jam is mostly set, 15–20 minutes.
Do ahead: Cookies can be assembled 3 days ahead. (The jam will cause the cookies to soften over time, but they will still be delicious.) Stack in an airtight container, separating layers with sheets of parchment paper. Store at room temperature. Uncoated, unfilled cookies can be stored in an airtight container for up to 1 day. Coat in the raspberry sugar and fill with jam before serving.


Editor’s note: Head this way for more of our favorite Christmas cookie recipes →

raspberry-tahini-thumbprint-cookies.name: Raspberry-tahini Thumbprint Cookies

ingredients.item: . diamond crystal or  0.5 tsp. morton kosher salt

instructions: Place racks in upper and lower thirds of oven; preheat to 375°. Whisk flour, baking powder, and salt in a medium bowl to combine. Beat egg, granulated sugar, brown sugar, butter, and almond extract (if using) in the bowl of a stand mixer fitted with the paddle attachment on medium speed until light and fluffy, about 3 minutes. Add tahini and beat, scraping down sides of bowl as needed, until combined. Reduce speed to low and add dry ingredients in 2 additions, mixing until completely combined after each addition. (Dough will be soft at this point.) Cover bowl and chill dough 30 minutes.

Mix black and white sesame seeds in a shallow bowl or dish. Working one at a time, scoop out dough by the tablespoonful and roll into balls. Roll each ball in sesame seeds to coat and divide between 2 parchment-lined baking sheets, spacing about 2" apart.

Bake cookies until slightly puffed and spread, 6–8 minutes. Remove from oven and press handle of a whisk or wooden spoon about halfway down into the center of each cookie to create a small well (the cookies will be too hot to use your thumb). Dollop about ½ tsp. jam into indentation in each cookie. Return cookies to oven, rotating baking sheets top to bottom and front to back, and bake until golden around edges and jam is beginning to set, 6–8 minutes. Let cookies cool on baking sheets before eating. (They will be soft right out of the oven but will firm up as they cool.)
Do ahead: Cookies can baked 1 day ahead. Store airtight at room temperature.

really-steak-marinade.name: Really Steak Marinade
instructions: Stir salad dressing, barbeque sauce, garlic, soya sauce, lemon juice, steak seasoning, seasoned salt, onion powder, and sugar in a bowl until the seasonings dissolve into the liquids.

recipe-for-chicken-thighs-en-papillote-w.name: Recipe For Chicken Thighs En Papillote With Vegetables
recipe-for-chicken-thighs-en-papillote-w.original_name: Receta de muslos de pollo en papillote con verduras

ingredients.item: 4 cdas  de aceite de oliva ( virgen extra)

instructions: Wash and cut all the vegetables as indicated. It purifies the oven to 200 been 3 been (199 been 3).
Coloca una hoja de papel aluminio o pergamino sobre una superficie plana.
At the centre, it lays a base of carrots, pumpkins, peppers, onion and mushrooms.
Coloca un muslo de pollo encima de las verduras.
Roast the chicken and vegetables with a tablespoon of olive oil, some lemon juice, salt, pepper, chopped garlic, a thyme and a rosemary.
Fold the foil or scroll into a hermetic envelope. Be sure to seal the edges well to prevent steam from escaping.
Coloca los papillotes en una bandeja para horno. Cocina durante 30-35 minutos.
Coloca cada papillote en un plato y permite que cada comensal lo abra. Disfruta el aroma que se libera al abrir el envoltorio.

recipe-for-peppers-stuffed-with-prawns-c.name: Recipe For Peppers Stuffed With Prawns
recipe-for-peppers-stuffed-with-prawns-c.original_name: Receta de pimientos rellenos de langostinos

ingredients.item: 300 gr de langostinos frescos ((pelados y limpios))

instructions: Lava bien los pimientos y corta la parte superior, retirando las semillas y las membranas internas. Reserva.
In a frying pan, heat up olive oil with medium heat and fry onion and garlic till clear. Add the minced tomato and cook until broken and form a thick sauce.

Add the locusts to the frying pan with salsa and saltle for about 3-4 minutes or until pink and baked. Take them from the fire and mix them with the baked rice. Adjust the salt and pepper point and add the chopped parsley.
Carefully fill up the peppers with a mixture of locusts and rice. If you want a more creamy texture, you can add some milk cream to the padded before closing them.

Put the peppers into a plate to bake previously frosted. Sprinkle some grated cheese onto each. Hornea in the pressurized oven at 180 been 3 been for 25-30 minutes or till the peppers are tender and the cheese golden.
Deja reposar unos minutos antes de servir.

recipe-for-traditional-and-very-shrimp-c.name: Recipe For Traditional And Very Shrimp Croquettes
recipe-for-traditional-and-very-shrimp-c.original_name: Receta de croquetas de gambas tradicionales y muy fáciles
instructions: To start with, we'll have to have the shrimp pitted. Otherwise, this is the perfect time to skin them. We'll also have to use them to cut them into pieces.
We'll have to reserve the prawns for now to get care of the onion. Let's get her peeling and pounding her into very small pieces.
Then we'll warm up an oil jet in a frying pan at medium-low heat. When it's hot, we'll use it to cook the chopped onion.
We'll cook it for 5 minutes, often stirring, with care that it doesn't get dark and stick to the frying pan surface.
Once this time has been passed, we will have to put in the pieces of shrimp. We'll have them pitted for another two minutes.
Then we'll add the flour to the frying pan and let it cook for two minutes, so that it will be felt very slightly.

After this time, we will pour milk and we will pat to taste. We will mix everything well, using a kitchen spatula, with the aim that the flour will be diluted into milk, do not stick to the frying pan and above all, that they will have no clumps.
Now we'll have to wait till the milk's hot, till it's broken to boiling. That will get our mix thick.
We can wait two minutes for the mixture to weigh more and then we will put out the fire and put it onto a fountain or container, so that it will rest. Now we have to wait till it's cold.
Once the cold mixture, it will have to have a context that will allow us to make croquetes.
When the dough gets cold, we will prepare a bowl with the contents of the eggs. We'll quickly batilate this mixture with the help of a manual mixer.
We will also have a plate covered with a plate of frosted bread.
Then, in a large and clear frying pan, we will warm up an abundant jet of oil. While it's hot, we'll get our necks worked up with our hands from the cheesebawk.
A medida que las croquetas vayan estando listas, las iremos pasando por el huevo y luego por el pan rallado, cuidando que queden bien rebozadas.

Enseguida, iremos colocando las croquetas en el aceite caliente. Las freiremos lo suficiente para que se doren por toda su superficie, pero queden suaves al interior. Si hace falta, iremos friendo las croquetas de gambas en varias tandas.
Optional, you can place the croquetes onto a plate covered with an absorbing paper sheet just as you get them out of the oil. That way, you'll remove excess fat.
That's all. The shrimp croquetes are ready to serve.

recipe-to-prepare-cinnamon-tea-comedera.name: Recipe To Prepare Cinnamon Tea

Let him boil
Serve and sweetens your taste with sugar, honey, chewed, sugars or anything you like best

red-chile-mop.name: Red Chile Mop

ingredients.item: Red chile hot sauce or store-bought medium hot sauce

instructions: Mix hot sauce, vinegar, and brown sugar in a small bowl to combine. Season with salt.

red-lentils-with-rice-and-vegetables-arg.name: Red Lentils With Rice And Vegetables
red-lentils-with-rice-and-vegetables-arg.original_name: Κόκκινες φακές με ρύζι και λαχανικά
instructions: Add rice and lentils to a colander and rinse them thoroughly under running water. We drain them.
Heat the olive oil in a deep pan and sauté the onion for 5-6 minutes to soften.
Add the tomato and continue the sauce for 3 more minutes.
Then pour the rice with the lentils and mix.
Dissolve the paste in the water or broth and pour the ingredients. Salt and pepper.
Once it boils, mix and completely lower the heat. Materials should be boiled without boiling in very low heat, as if steamed.
Cover the vessel and leave the food on fire for 15 minutes until it drinks its liquids.
However, heat the remaining olive oil (2 tabletspoons) in a pan and saute the opinions in a very low heat for 10 minutes to caramelize.
Add the cumin and garlic and sauté for 1-2 minutes until slightly golden.
Pour with the mixture the ingredients of the pot and mix.
Sprinkle with chopped parsley.
Apply to shallow bowls of herbal yogurt and serve on top the lens while still warm.

red-snapper-and-shrimp-ceviche-with-avoc.name: Red Snapper And Shrimp Ceviche With Avocado

ingredients.prep: boneless red snapper fillet, cut into  0.5" pieces
halved lengthwise, or small shrimp, peeled, deveined,  cut into  0.5" pieces

ingredients.item: Canned piquillo peppers or roasted red peppers from a jar

instructions: Combine shallot, lime juice, orange juice, and red pepper flakes in a large bowl. Add red snapper and shrimp, season with salt, and toss gently to combine. Let sit, tossing occasionally, until fish firms up slightly, 20–25 minutes.
Add avocados, mango, hearts of palm, piquillo peppers, herbs, and oil to ceviche and toss to coat. Taste and season with more salt and/or lime juice if desired.

refrigerator-dessert-with-petit-ber-argi.name: Refrigerator Dessert With Petit Ber

ingredients.item: 8 tablespoons full vanilla maize blossom (or cornflower)
8 tablespoons full vanilla maize blossom (or cornflower)

instructions: Keep 1/2 liters of milk separate and dissolve the corn flour.
Place the remaining milk in a pot with the sugar to heat.
Then pour the cold milk with the cornflower into the pot with the hot milk.
Mix on medium heat until the cream thickens.
After it thickens, add the butter and mix to melt.
Divide it into two bowls while still warm.
Add the chocolate praline grated and mix well.
In the other half, mix the white chocolate.
[adrotate banner=”33′′]
Mix the creams with spatula while warm to melt the chocolates.
In bowls, crush into thick pieces the pitty ber cookies and add the wafers.
Sprinkle them with liquor and mix them.
Add half the biscuits to the chocolate cream and mix to dress.
Pour them into a pyre in a layer.
Add the remaining cookies to the white chocolate cream and mix to dress.
Pour them into a layer on the chocolate cream.
Freeze until it cools completely.
Serve with finely ground wafers and truffles.

revani-with-cocoa-and-whipped-cream-lezz.name: Revani With Cocoa And Whipped Cream
instructions: 1. Leave water and sugar in the pot and boil in the middle fire.
2. Add lemon juice after boiling for a few minutes.
3. Leave to cool from January.
4. Set oven to 180 degrees.
5. Cook the butter for 6-7 minutes with mixer, taking yogurt, eggs, broths and sugary vanilla into the mixing container.
6. Add flour, dough baking powder and cocoa and mix it with spatula.
7. Transfer the dough into lubricated oven container and cook for 35-40 minutes in the oven.
8. After taking the oven and waiting for 1 minute, visit the cold harvest.
9. Leave to rest for 1 hour.
10. Spray the milk until the cream is joined with schanti and broadcast on the dessert.
11. 11. Serve and serve Kakao.
12. Click for the simple revani recipe.

rib-eye-with-bone-marrow-and-radish-sala.name: Rib Eye With Bone Marrow And Radish Salad
instructions: Season steak with kosher salt and pepper. Let sit at room temperature 2 hours, or chill up to 12 hours.

Preheat oven to 300°. Heat vegetable oil in a large ovenproof skillet, preferably cast-iron, over medium-high. Cook steak until deep golden brown, 5–8 minutes per side. Add butter and spoon over steak a few times. Transfer skillet to oven and roast steak until an instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest part registers 120° for rare, 5–8 minutes. Transfer steak to a cutting board and let rest. Increase oven temperature to 425°.

Combine anchovies, capers, and olive oil in a small bowl; season with kosher salt and pepper. Spread half of anchovy mixture onto cut sides of marrow bone. Roast bone on a rimmed baking sheet until top is browned and marrow is tender, 10–12 minutes.

Meanwhile, cut half of the radishes into quarters (leave the tops on, if using); trim and thinly slice remaining radishes crosswise. Toss radishes, celery leaves, parsley, lemon juice, and remaining anchovy mixture in a medium bowl. Season radish salad with kosher salt, pepper, and more lemon juice, if desired.
Cut steak off bone and slice ½" thick. Transfer meat to a platter, pour pan juices over, and sprinkle with sea salt. Serve alongside marrow bone, radish salad, and toast.

ribollita.name: Ribollita
instructions: Place a rack in the middle of the oven; preheat to 232°C (232°C).
Prepare your mirepoix: cut 1 large onion in half through the root, peel, and finely chop. Transfer to a medium bowl.
Wash, peel, and trim 3 medium carrots. Cut in half (or in quarters lengthwise if large), then cut crosswise into ½" pieces. Add to the bowl with the onion.
Chop 2 celery stalks crosswise into ½" pieces. Add to the bowl with the rest of the mirepoix.
Smash and peel 8 garlic cloves with the back of your knife until they open up and break apart. Add to the bowl.
Strip stems from 2 bunches Tuscan kale and discard. Wash the leaves if gritty, then tear into 2" pieces; set aside.
Cut the rind off of 1 small Parmesan wedge; set aside.
Place 28 oz. canned tomatoes and their juices in a strainer set inside a medium bowl (to collect the juice). Squeeze and crush tomatoes with your hands while leaving them submerged in their liquid.
Tear ½ loaf crusty country bread into 1½" pieces.

Heat ⅓ cup extra-virgin olive oil in a medium Dutch oven or heavy pot over medium heat. Add the bowl of mirepoix and 2 tsp. salt. Cook, stirring occasionally, until vegetables are softened but not browned, 8–10 minutes.

Lift the strainer full of tomatoes and shake to remove any liquid. Add the crushed tomato solids (but not the reserved juices!) to the pot and cook, stirring occasionally, until some of the rawness is cooked off, about 10 minutes. (Remember to reserve the tomato juices.)

While tomatoes are cooking, drain and rinse 14 oz. canned cannellini beans in the strainer you just used. Add the beans to the pot along with the Parmesan rind, reserved tomato juices, ¾ tsp. red pepper flakes, and 4 cups water. Bring to a simmer.
Add the kale in two additions, stirring often and allowing to wilt in between.
Add about one-third of the torn bread (eyeball it) and cook, stirring occasionally, until coated and warmed through, about 5 minutes more. Taste and adjust seasoning.
Remove from heat. Place the remaining torn bread chunks on top of the stew. Drizzle generously with olive oil.
Transfer the pot to the oven and bake until the stew is thick, bubbling, and the bread is golden brown on top, 10–15 minutes.
Ladle the stew into bowls, drizzle each generously with olive oil, and grate lots of Parmesan over.

rice-recipe-with-artichokes-and-lemon-co.name: Rice Recipe With Artichokes And Lemon
rice-recipe-with-artichokes-and-lemon-co.original_name: Receta de arroz con alcachofas y limón

ingredients.item: 397 g corazones de alcachofa enlatada en cuartos (1 lata de 14oz)
1 taza arroz blanco de grano largo

instructions: Ralla and print the lemon juice. You will require 2 tablespoons of juice and ½ tablespoons of lemon (and extra to decorate if you wish).
Escurre y pica los corazones de alcachofa. Reserva.
In your frying pan with medium heat, add oil and hot. Add garlic, oregano, pits and salt. Jumps for 1 minute.
Add the hearts of artichoke, rice, broth, lemon juice and pounding. Revels slightly to combine the ingredients. Cover and come up with medium-high fire.
Espera que el caldo comience a hervir y luego baja la flama a fuego bajo para calentar a fuego lento. Deja el que arroz hierva durante 15 minutos destapar ni revolver.
Remove the frying pan from the fire and leave to stand for 5 minutes.
Destapa y esponja el arroz con un tenedor. Distribuye la alcachofa por todo el arroz.
Serve and decorate with feta and parsley cheese if you wish, enjoy.

risotto-milanese-argiro.name: Risotto Milanese

ingredients.item: 11⁄2 lthot chicken broth (home or trade or 2 fresh refrigerator Cubes)
11⁄2 lthot chicken broth (home or trade or 2 fresh refrigerator Cubes)

instructions: Add the saffron to 1/2 cup of warm broth and let it stand and take off its perfume and color.
Heat the butter in a pan over medium to high heat.
Add the chopped dry onion and sauce for about 3-4 minutes, stirring. Onion should not take dark color.
Add the rice and stir for 1 minute to make the rice grain transparent and polish with the butter.
Put out the ingredients with the wine and let all the alcohol evaporate by imprisoning its aromas.
When the pan is dried from the wine, start gradually pouring the warm chicken broth. Each time the rice absorbs the liquids, add the next dose of hot broth.
Mix the risotto regularly with a wooden spoon or tan.
In the middle of cooking, 10 minutes after the start of cooking, add the saffron.
Mix by watching risotto take the golden color of the saffron and release its precious perfume.
Continue cooking for 5′, adding broth each time the rice absorbs it.
Add salt and pepper to the risotto and boil for about 2 minutes.
At this stage about 17-18 minutes from the time you started, the rice will be poured into the pan but its heart will hold a little, it will be al-dente.
Remove risotto from fire.
Add the butter into pieces as well as the grated parmesan.
Stir continuously for a few seconds until the butter melts and the parmesan and risotto become creamy and delicious.
Serve the risotto immediately, sprinkle it with some chives and some freshly ground pepper.
Remember, as long as the risotto stays on the plates, it keeps baking for the next two minutes and sucking its fluids. So enjoy the delicious risotto that spoke warm and creamy, just as it should.

roast-chickens-with-pistachio-salsa-pepp.name: Roast Chickens With Pistachio Salsa, Peppers, And Corn

ingredients.prep: minced, mashed to a paste with the back of a knife

ingredients.item: Chopped fresh thyme leaves plus sprigs for stuffing
Minced capers plus 1–2 teaspoons caper brine

ingredients.prep: orange, and red bell peppers (about 5 large peppers)

instructions: Whisk lemon juice, oil, garlic, 3 Tbsp. thyme, rosemary, and sage in a small bowl; set marinade aside.

Season chickens with salt and pepper inside and out. Place each inside a resealable plastic bag; divide marinade between bags. Spread marinade evenly to distribute. Seal bags and chill for at least 4 hours and up to 24 hours.

Preheat oven to 450°. Transfer chickens from bags to a small roasting pan; gently blot excess oil from marinade with paper towels, leaving herbs intact on chicken skin. Stuff each chicken with a lemon half and several thyme sprigs. Tie legs together with kitchen twine, if desired.

Roast until well browned, 30–35 minutes. Reduce heat to 350° and continue roasting, occasionally basting chickens with pan juices, until an instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest part of the thigh registers 165°, 20–30 minutes longer. Transfer chickens to a carving board; let rest for 20 minutes before carving.

Heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add onion, capers, and red pepper flakes. Season with salt and cook, stirring occasionally, until onion is soft but not brown, about 5 minutes. Add peppers and chiles; season with salt and cook, stirring occasionally, until soft, 10–12 minutes. Stir in caper brine, corn, parsley, and vinegar just before serving.
Combine first 4 ingredients in a small bowl. Stir in oil. DO AHEAD Can be made 2 days ahead. Cover and chill. Let come to room temperature before serving. Serve with chicken, peppers and corn.

roast-salmon-with-citrus-and-coconut-chi.name: Roast Salmon With Citrus And Coconut-chile Crunch

ingredients.prep: red jalapeños, or red serrano chiles (green is okay if that is what you can find), thinly sliced

ingredients.item: . white balsamic vinegar or seasoned rice vinegar

instructions: Preheat oven to 350°. Pat salmon dry, then season with kosher salt. Heat 1 Tbsp. oil in a large ovenproof nonstick skillet over medium. Cook salmon (skin side down if it has skin) until well browned underneath, 6–8 minutes, then transfer to oven. Roast until flesh is opaque and flakes easily with a fork, 8–12 minutes.

Meanwhile, cook chiles, ginger, coconut flakes, peanuts, and remaining 5 Tbsp. oil in a small saucepan over medium heat, stirring often, until coconut and peanuts are golden, 8–10 minutes. Transfer coconut-chile crunch to a heatproof bowl and let cool slightly.

Using a small sharp knife, cut peel and white pith from citrus, leaving as much flesh behind as possible. Cut flesh from cores in a variety of shapes and sizes and arrange on plates. Break salmon into big pieces and divide among plates, arranging on top of citrus.
Stir vinegar into coconut-chile crunch and season with salt (it will probably need a few pinches). Spoon over salad and sprinkle with sea salt.

roasted-carrots-with-cumin-yogurt.name: Roasted Carrots With Cumin Yogurt

ingredients.item: Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper

instructions: Preheat oven to 450°. Toss carrots, orange juice, thyme, bay leaves, and ¼ cup oil on a rimmed baking sheet; season with salt and pepper. Roast, tossing halfway through, until golden brown and soft, 30–35 minutes; remove bay leaves.
Meanwhile, toast coriander in a small dry skillet over medium-high heat, tossing, until fragrant, about 1 minute; transfer to a plate. Repeat with cumin. Let cool; coarsely chop.
Purée coriander, cumin, yogurt, lime juice, ¼ cup cilantro, and remaining 2 Tbsp. oil in a food processor until smooth; season with salt and pepper.
Serve carrots topped with cumin yogurt, cilantro, and sesame seeds.

roasted-eggplant-and-bell-pepper-salad.name: Roasted Eggplant And Bell Pepper Salad

ingredients.item: Salt and ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C).
Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add onion and cook, stirring, until browned, 5 to 7 minutes. Transfer to a plate.

Add remaining olive oil to the skillet; add eggplant and bell pepper. Cook, stirring, until softened, about 5 minutes. Add tomato, garlic, parsley, salt, and pepper; cook 2 to 3 minutes more. Return onion to the skillet. Adjust seasonings if necessary.
Transfer eggplant mixture to a baking pan. Bake in the preheated oven for 30 minutes.
Cool 1 hour; serve at room temperature or refrigerate and serve chilled.

roasted-grape-pavlova-bites.name: Roasted Grape Pavlova Bites
instructions: Preheat oven to 250°. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper. Whisk sugar and cornstarch in a small bowl.

Using an electric mixer on medium speed, beat egg whites, cream of tartar, and salt in a large bowl until soft peaks form. Increase speed to medium-high and gradually beat in sugar mixture. Add Cognac and continue beating until mixture is glossy and stiff peaks form.
Spoon teaspoonfuls onto prepared baking sheet, forming 24 small meringues. Use the back of a spoon or a teaspoon measure to create an indentation in the center of each.

Bake until meringues are crisp and no longer sticky, 60–75 minutes. Transfer parchment paper with meringues to a wire rack to cool completely. DO AHEAD: Can be made 1 day ahead. Store airtight at room temperature.

Preheat oven to 500°. Place grapes on a large rimmed baking sheet lined with parchment paper; drizzle with honey; toss to coat. Roast, stirring occasionally, until grapes begin to burst, 8–10 minutes. Transfer grapes on sheet to a wire rack to cool. DO AHEAD: Can be made 4 hours ahead. Let stand at room temperature.

Whisk crème fraîche and cream in a medium bowl until soft peaks form. Place a dollop of cream mixture in indentations in meringue centers. Top each with 2–3 roasted grapes. Sprinkle cracked black pepper over and serve immediately

roasted-turnips.name: Roasted Turnips

ingredients.item: Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Preheat the oven to 204°C (200 degrees C).

Peel and trim turnips. Then cut into 1-1/4 inch pieces and place into a bowl. Drizzle with olive oil and season with thyme, salt, and pepper; toss until turnips are well coated. Spread out in 1 layer on a large baking sheet.
Roast for 15 minutes. Turn and roast until fork-tender and lightly browned, 10 to 15 more minutes.

roasted-veal-shanks-with-rosemary.name: Roasted Veal Shanks With Rosemary
instructions: Preheat oven to 246°C. Sprinkle veal with coarse salt and black pepper. Heat 4 tablespoons oil in heavy large ovenproof pot over medium-high heat. Add veal; cook until dark brown, turning occasionally, 25 to 30 minutes. Transfer pot to oven; roast uncovered 30 minutes (veal will be dark brown). Maintain oven temperature.

Meanwhile, heat 2 tablespoons oil in heavy large skillet over medium-high heat. Add carrots, onion, and celery; sauté until softened, about 12 minutes. Add chopped rosemary, sage, and garlic; sauté 2 minutes. Transfer veal to plate; pour off oil from pot. Add wine, 2 cups broth, and vegetables to pot and bring to boil. Return veal to pot (broth will not cover veal). Roast uncovered until meat is very tender, turning every 30 minutes and adding more broth by cupfuls as needed to moisten as broth evaporates, about 1 hour 15 minutes.

Transfer veal to large plate. Set strainer over medium bowl. Pour juices and vegetables from pot into strainer, pressing on solids to extract liquid. Transfer vegetables to processor; puree until almost smooth. Skim fat from juices in bowl; discard fat. Whisk 1 cup vegetable puree into juices in bowl (discard remaining puree). Season with coarse salt and black pepper. Return puree mixture and veal to same pot. Return to oven and roast 10 minutes longer, turning veal once.
Cut meat off bones into 1-inch-thick pieces. Transfer to bowl. Pour sauce over. Garnish with parsley and rosemary sprigs.

rock-shrimp-pasta-with-spicy-tomato-sauc.name: Rock Shrimp Pasta With Spicy Tomato Sauce

ingredients.prep: fronds reserved, core removed, bulb thinly sliced

instructions: Set a fine-mesh sieve over a medium bowl. Working over sieve, squeeze tomatoes to release juices and break up flesh. Let tomatoes drain in sieve, collecting juices in bowl, until ready to use.

Heat ⅓ cup oil in a large Dutch oven or other heavy pot over medium. Cook fennel, garlic, and half of chile, stirring often, until garlic is golden and fennel is starting to brown around the edges, 5–8 minutes. Transfer fennel mixture with a slotted spoon to a small bowl.

Increase heat to high; cook drained tomatoes in same pot, stirring occasionally, until broken down and starting to brown, about 5 minutes. Add wine, scraping up browned bits, and cook until almost completely evaporated, about 1 minute. Add fennel mixture and collected tomato juices, bring to a simmer, and cook, stirring occasionally, until slightly thickened, 5–10 minutes.
Meanwhile, cook pasta in a large pot of boiling salted water, stirring occasionally, until very al dente, about 3 minutes less than package directions.

Using a spider or a slotted spoon, transfer pasta to pot with sauce; add shrimp and ½ cup pasta cooking liquid. Cook, tossing often, until pasta is al dente and coated in sauce and shrimp are cooked through, about 3 minutes. Thin sauce with more pasta cooking liquid if needed; season with salt. Remove from heat; add butter and toss to combine. Season with more salt if needed.
Divide pasta among bowls. Top with fennel fronds and remaining chile and drizzle with oil.

rose-petal-cream-lezzet.name: Rose Petal Cream
instructions: 1. The sweet recipes that can be applied easily in the upper row.
2. Heat the milk and 2 tablespoons of sugar until thoroughly melt.
3. Add rose water and get from the stove.
4. Add and mix cheese yeast.
5. Transfer it to a deep service container.
6. Keep it up to 2-3 hours of slip.
7. Mix the remaining sugar into the cream and slowly pour it on the mixture with milk.
8. Serve by decorating with rose leaves.

rum-and-raisin-banana-smoothie.name: Rum And Raisin Banana Smoothie
instructions: Blend almond milk, banana, rum, honey, raisins, and nutmeg in a blender until smooth, about 30 seconds.

russian-compound-butter.name: Russian Compound Butter
instructions: Process all ingredients in a food processor until smooth.
DO AHEAD: Butter can be made 5 days ahead. Wrap tightly and chill, or freeze up to 1 month.

russian-poppy-seed-roll.name: Russian Poppy Seed Roll
instructions: Combine warm milk, yeast, and 1 teaspoon sugar in a large bowl; stir well. Let stand until yeast softens and begins to form a bubbly foam, about 5 minutes.

Stir 1/2 cup sugar and lemon zest into the yeast mixture. Whisk in 2 eggs and egg yolks, 1 at time, mixing well after each addition. Add salt. Stir in flour gradually. Add melted butter and blend well. Knead dough until glossy and elastic, about 10 minutes. Form into a ball and place in a large, lightly-oiled bowl and turn to coat. Cover with a clean towel and let rise in a warm place until doubled in volume, about 1 hour.

Place poppy seeds in a small pot and cover with 1 cup milk or more as needed. Bring to a simmer over medium heat. Remove from heat and let sit, 15 to 20 minutes. Drain milk. Pulse poppy seeds in a food processor, or leave them as is. Add raisins, egg whites, honey, 1/4 cup sugar, walnuts, and 3 1/2 tablespoons butter to poppy seeds and stir to combine for the filling.

Punch down dough, turn out onto a lightly floured surface, and knead briefly. Divide dough in half and roll one portion into a 10-inch sheet. Cover with 1/2 the filling, leaving a 1/4- to 1/2-inch edge. Roll up into a log. Repeat with the second half.
Arrange both rolls onto a greased baking sheet. Cover with a kitchen towel and let rise until doubled in volume, about 30 minutes more.
Preheat oven to 177°C (175 degrees C).
Whisk remaining egg with 1 tablespoon water; brush mixture over tops of rolls.
Bake rolls in the preheated oven until golden, 20 to 25 minutes.
